stories.title: Skubal Returns to the Mound — Tigers' Ace Back from IL After 38 Days
stories.angle: Two-time Cy Young winner Tarik Skubal (3-2, 2.70 ERA) makes his return to Detroit's rotation today at Progressive Field against the Guardians. He's been on the IL since May 6 following elbow surgery. Rehab start: 5 IP, 0 ER, 6 K. Tigers went 7-2 in June before today despite being without their ace. Every contender is watching — Skubal is a free agent after 2026 and the trade deadline is August 3.

article.headline: Skubal Returns: Tigers' Two-Time Cy Young Ace Back from IL Against Guardians

research.facts:
Skubal Returns to the Mound
**Sources:**
- ESPN: "Tarik Skubal returning to Tigers' rotation against Guardians" (espn.com/mlb/story/_/id/49030517) — confirmed return June 13 at Progressive Field vs Guardians (4:10 PM ET); Skubal 3-2, 2.70 ERA entering game
- NBC Sports: "2-time Cy Young winner Tarik Skubal is returning to Tigers' rotation against the Guardians" — IL stint since May 6 elbow surgery; 38 days; rehab start: 5 IP, 0 ER, 6 K
- Yahoo Sports: "Tigers ace Tarik Skubal to start Saturday's game against Guardians" — opponent LHP Joey Cantillo (4-3, 4.57 ERA) for Cleveland
- Pipeline status June 12: confirmed "Skubal starts Saturday June 13 at Cleveland/activated from IL/38-day elbow surgery/5 IP 0 ER 6K rehab/Tigers 7-2 June"
- Previous coverage: free agent after 2026, August 3 deadline implications, every contender watching
- Confidence: HIGH on return date/opponent/rehab stats (multiple independent sources)
**Freshness:** June 13 start is the featured event — return from IL. Game in progress/today.

stories.title: Alvarez Homers Twice, Ties Schwarber at 24 — Derby Race Is On
stories.angle: Yordan Alvarez crushed two home runs — including a first-inning grand slam — as the Astros beat the Royals 10-8 on June 13. Alvarez is now tied with Kyle Schwarber at 24 home runs to lead MLB. Both are ahead of Byron Buxton (21). The Home Run Derby is in Philadelphia on July 13 — Schwarber's home park — making the race even more compelling.

article.headline: Alvarez Homers Twice, Ties Schwarber at 24 — Home Run Race Officially On

- StatMuse: "Current MLB Home Run Leaders" — Alvarez and Schwarber tied at 24 HR; Buxton 3rd at 21 HR
- Previous coverage June 12: Schwarber 23 HR, Alvarez 22 HR (Schwarber had 1-HR lead)

- Confidence: HIGH on HR totals (multiple sources); HIGH on Astros 10-8 Royals result (confirmed)
**Freshness:** June 13 HR pace change (Alvarez ties Schwarber) — new tie at top of leaderboard.

stories.title: Braves 45-23 — Baseball's Best Team Is Playing Like It
stories.angle: Atlanta's record stands at 45-23, the best in baseball. A year after finishing 76-86, the Braves are on pace to challenge the single-season win record. Manager Walt Weiss (first full year) is getting elite performance from Chris Sale and Ronald Acuña Jr. leading the offense. The NL East lead is 8+ games.

article.headline: The Braves Are 45-23 and Baseball's Best Team — One Year After Going 76-86

stories.title: Judge Update — Mid-August Return, What It Means for Yankees' Deadline Math
stories.angle: Aaron Judge (right rib stress fracture) is expected to return in mid-August — after the August 3 trade deadline. Yankees manager Aaron Boone expressed strong confidence Judge will play this season. With Judge out, the Yankees are 41-26 and hold a narrow AL East lead. The question: do they buy aggressively at the deadline without knowing what they're getting with Judge, or trust their lead?

article.headline: Judge Update: Mid-August Return Expected — and What It Means for Yankees' Deadline Math

research.facts:
Judge — Mid-August Return
**Sources:**
- CBS Sports: "When will Aaron Judge be back? How can Yankees replace him?" — rib stress fracture; out since May 31; 2-month timeline = mid-August
- Yahoo Sports: "Aaron Judge's rib injury saga" — expected return this season, strong confidence from manager Boone
- Bleacher Report: "Aaron Judge's Injury Diagnosis, Return Timeline Revealed" — mid-August target
- Sportskeeda: "Could Aaron Judge return by All-Star break" — no, likely mid-August
- Yankees record: 41-26 (from standings search)
- AL East: Yankees 41-26, Rays 40-25
- August 3 = MLB trade deadline; mid-August return = 2 weeks after deadline
- Confidence: HIGH on injury type (multiple sources), HIGH on timeline (multiple independent sources), HIGH on Yankees record (standings search)
**Freshness:** Ongoing story — deadline math implications with mid-August return date.

stories.title: Trade Deadline Big Board — Seven Weeks Out, a Strong Sellers' Market
stories.angle: Seven weeks before the August 3 deadline, a preview of the top available chips. Chapman (Boston, 0.46 ERA, 17 saves), Mize (Detroit, 2.27 ERA, free agent after 2026), and Logan Webb (Giants, if San Francisco continues to rebuild) headline a strong seller's market. This sets up a potentially active deadline for contenders.

article.headline: Trade Deadline Big Board: Seven Weeks Out, Chapman and Mize Lead a Strong Seller's Market

- Bleacher Report: "Updated 2026 MLB Trade Deadline Big Board" — Chapman, Mize, Webb among top names
- Motorcitybengals.com (Tigers beat): Casey Mize mentioned as trade candidate — 2.27 ERA, free agent after 2026; Jeff Passan labeled key Detroit trade candidate
- SI.com: "The Top Trade Chips for Six Likely MLB Trade Deadline Sellers" — Giants' Logan Webb
- ESPN Jeff Passan: "Early 2026 MLB trade deadline preview" — Skubal, Tigers bullpen, multiple names
- August 3 deadline (7 weeks from June 13)
- Confidence: HIGH on Chapman stats (multiple sources), HIGH on Mize as candidate (multiple sources), MEDIUM on Webb (reported speculation, not confirmed move)
